layout.title: Frietkot · Belgian fry shop in Bourg-Argental · Since 1990
layout.description: Authentic Belgian fries, Trappist beers and daily homemade specials in Bourg-Argental, Loire. Inn on site.
layout.ogTitle: Frietkot — Belgian fry shop in Bourg-Argental
layout.ogDescription: Authentic Belgian fries, daily homemade specials, Trappist beers and an inn. Since 1990, in the heart of the Loire.
layout.ogImageAlt: Frietkot — Belgian fry shop in Bourg-Argental, Loire. Fresh house-made fries since 1990.
layout.twitterTitle: Frietkot — Belgian fry shop in Bourg-Argental

glaces.pageDescription: Twelve house-made glaces by Pascal: classics, signatures, indulgent flavours and fresh sorbets at Frietkot.
glaces.ogDescription: House-made glaces by Pascal: twelve flavours, four families, real ingredients.
glaces.heroEyebrow: THE GLACES · MADE FRESH EACH MORNING
glaces.heroTagline: Twelve flavours, four families, one simple method: whole milk, seasonal fruit and real ingredients.
glaces.intro: Pascal does not do things by halves. After the fries, there is the ice cream: made here, in honest quantities, with the clear flavour you recognise from the first spoonful.
glaces.teaserCompactTitle: Pascal keeps a few flavours cold.
glaces.teaserCompactBody: Twelve house-made glaces, from classics to fruit sorbets. Another reason to stop at the counter.

categoryContexts.classique: The foundation, straight and clean: the flavours people order with their eyes closed, made to let the taste speak.
categoryContexts.signature: Pascal's house creations, a little more distinctive, with the extra character regulars come back for.
categoryContexts.gourmand: Rounder, richer flavours, made to finish the meal or sit beside a warm Liège waffle.
categoryContexts.sorbet: Fresh fruit, bright freshness and just enough sweetness to keep summer in the cone.

flavorDescriptions.vanille: Soft, rounded vanilla, made for simple cones and no-fuss endings.
flavorDescriptions.chocolat: A direct chocolate, dark enough, with a dense texture that holds on the spoon.
flavorDescriptions.fraise: Strawberry as a classic: fruity, fresh, familiar and ready for a warm waffle.
flavorDescriptions.pistache: Pistachio with character, green without cheating, with the clear taste of the nut.
flavorDescriptions.speculoos: The Belgian flavour: biscuit, gentle spice and a properly indulgent finish.
flavorDescriptions.caramelSale: Salted butter caramel, rich and precise, the one that clings a little to the spoon.
flavorDescriptions.stracciatella: Fresh cream and chocolate shards, simple at first glance, generous every bite.
flavorDescriptions.noisette: Rounded, toasted hazelnut, perfect when you want something deeper.
flavorDescriptions.cafe: Clean coffee, not too sweet, for those who like a more grown-up finish.
flavorDescriptions.citron: Bright and clean, a lemon sorbet that wakes the palate without weighing it down.
flavorDescriptions.framboise: Fresh raspberry, gently tart, with the lift of red fruit that makes you come back.
flavorDescriptions.mangue: Sunny mango, supple and fragrant, the sorbet everyone can agree on.

glaces.closingNote: The ice cream menu evolves with the seasons. In summer, Pascal adds seasonal fruit flavours. In winter, he brings out richer speculoos and caramels.
glaces.closingCta: ASK PASCAL WHAT HE HAS TODAY

home.metaDescription: Premium Belgian fry shop in Bourg-Argental: house fries, Belgian beers and guest rooms.
home.pascalImgAlt: Pascal, owner of Frietkot in Bourg-Argental, welcomes you to his Belgian fry shop with a smile
home.pascalBody: Thirty-five years in the trade, one philosophy: authentic Belgian fries, simple hospitality, a welcome with a smile. Meet us at 7 place d'Armeville, at the counter.
home.belgiumTitle2: in the heart of the Loire.
home.belgiumBody: Fries cut and cooked on site. Trappist beers hand-picked. An inn to make the night last. Here we don’t cut corners — we treat you right.
home.metierTitle: The art of the fry, made in-house.
home.metierP1: Every morning, potatoes are peeled, cut, and dipped twice in our fryers — as it should be. A first gentle cook, a rest, then the final fry. It’s that double cook, and nothing else, that gives Belgian fries a melting heart and a crackling crust.
home.metierP2: No frozen shortcut. No compromise. Just the method learned in Brussels, passed on since 1990.
home.saucesImgAlt: Homemade sauces and Belgian fry shop at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — a generous line-up of accompaniments made every morning
home.carrouselDesc: A glimpse of our essentials, in an authentic friterie spirit.
home.car1Body: Regular cone, large cone, extra. Homemade mayo, andalouse, samurai, garlic, mustard. Wide choice, one foundation.
home.car2Body: Slow-cooked carbonnade, vol-au-vent, Liège-style meatballs. Belgian classics prepared each morning from what the market offers.
home.car3Body: Fresh salads (chicken, tuna, salmon) for lighter meals. And to finish — Liège waffles, crème brûlée, artisan ice cream.
home.biereDesc: A Belgian fry shop without a Belgian beer is only half the story.
home.biereP1: We keep the list short but right — Trappist ales, strong blondes, abbey brunes. Each picked to match a dish, a season, a moment.
home.biereP2: Ask at the counter — we’ll point you to a bottle.
home.biereImgAlt: Belgian beer selection at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — Chimay, Duvel, Paljas, Herberg and craft bottles at the counter
home.innP1: Steps from the fry shop, we open our rooms. Three comfortable, simple spaces with a Belgian feel — a nod to Duvel, to Paljas, to northern conviviality.
home.innP2: For a food-focused getaway, a weekend in the Pilat, or a stop on the road south.
home.hoursLine: To be confirmed with the owner
home.reviewsWidget: Google Reviews widget · 4.5 ★ / 308 reviews (integration to connect)

hero.subtitle: Potatoes peeled by hand, cut on site, double-fried — as it should be. For thirty-five years.
hero.videoAria: Hand-cut Belgian fries being prepared at Frietkot

laCarte.pageDescription: Belgian fries, daily specials, homemade sauces, waffles and Belgian beers.
laCarte.ogDescription: Belgian fries, daily homemade specials, sauces, waffles and Belgian beers in Bourg-Argental.
laCarte.heroImgAlt: Daily specials at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — carbonade with brown beer, mushroom vol-au-vent, and Liège-style meatballs, all made in-house every day
laCarte.intro: Three Belgian classics, prepared each morning from what the market offers. Always served with fresh fries, cut on site.
laCarte.carbonadeBody: Braised in Belgian brown ale, caramelised onions, gingerbread, mustard as a filigree. Slow cooking, a generous plate, house fries on the side.
laCarte.carbonadeImgAlt: Carbonnade flamande with brown beer, slow-braised, served at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental with crisp Belgian fries
laCarte.volBody: Golden puff pastry, a tight cream, seasonal mushrooms and farm chicken. A classic served hot — Belgian fries complete the plate.
laCarte.volImgAlt: Homemade vol-au-vent at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — puff pastry, cream and mushrooms, generous presentation
laCarte.bouletsHeadline: Generous, sweet-savory, from the north to the cone.
laCarte.bouletsBody: Hand-shaped beef balls, a long-cooked onion sauce, a hint of Liège syrup. A brasserie plate to share with Trappist fries in reach.
laCarte.bouletsImgAlt: Liège-style meatballs on the daily specials at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — with Belgian fries
laCarte.saucesTitle: Made in the morning, served the moment you dip.
laCarte.saucesBody: Texture, balance, gentle heat — the details matter. No shortcuts: everything’s ready to meet your cone of the day.
laCarte.saucesImgAlt: Homemade sauce bar at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — colorful bowls, creamy textures, neat display at the counter
laCarte.pricingSubtitle: Price guide — daily specials and suggestions are on the board each morning.
laCarte.saucesListBody: Homemade mayo, andalouse, samurai, garlic, mustard, ketchup, tartare.
laCarte.callout: A question about a dish? Call us directly.

glacesCta.title: And our glaces?
glacesCta.text: Pascal also prepares twelve house flavours, from classics to fruit sorbets.

auberge.pageDescription: Our guest rooms in Bourg-Argental: Delirium Room, Duvel Room and Queue de Charrue Room.
auberge.ogDescription: Three Belgian rooms steps from the fry shop. For a food-focused break in the Pilat.
auberge.metaTitleThemed: The Frietkot Inn — 3 themed Belgian rooms in Bourg-Argental
auberge.metaDescriptionThemed: Delirium, Duvel and Queue de Charrue rooms — three guest rooms inspired by iconic Belgian beers, steps from our friterie.
auberge.heroSubtitle: Just steps from the friterie, extend your evening in one of our three guest rooms — each inspired by an iconic Belgian beer.
auberge.heroConcept: Here, you don't sleep in a numbered room. You sleep in a story.
auberge.ctaBody: Book by phone or email — we always reply within the day. From €65 per night, breakfast included.
auberge.ctaNote: Rooms are available year-round, except for the few days we close the friterie for holidays.

delirium.name: The Delirium room
delirium.beerStory: Born in 1988 at Huyghe Brewery, Delirium Tremens became a global icon — recognisable by its stoneware bottle and pink elephants. The Delirium Café in Brussels holds the Guinness World Record for the bar offering the most beers in the world (over 2,000).
delirium.roomStory: A room full of character, in the extravagant spirit of the brand. Warm colours, flamboyant pink touches, references to Delirium's iconography on the walls — the famous enamelled metal signs.
delirium.atmosphere: A room for evenings that don't get retold. The kind where you laugh too loud, where you talk about everything and nothing until dawn.

duvel.name: The Duvel room
duvel.beerStory: "Duvel" means "devil" in Flemish — the name given by a taster in 1923 faced with the power of this strong golden blonde. Brewed by Duvel Moortgat, it is served in its signature tulip glass, dense foam, 8.5% well hidden.
duvel.roomStory: Deep turquoise walls, vintage enamelled Duvel signs, red-and-white bedding in the brand's colours. A direct, honest room with the character of the beer that gives it its name.
duvel.atmosphere: Like the beer: you don't see it coming, and then it marks you. Sleep well — you won't see the morning arrive.

queueDeCharrue.name: The Queue de Charrue room
queueDeCharrue.imageAlt: Queue de Charrue–themed guest room, Frietkot Bourg-Argental
queueDeCharrue.beerStory: "Queue de Charrue" is the French translation of the Flemish village Ploegsteert, home of the Vanuxeem Brewery. A family of brewers since 1906, who in 1986 revived a traditional brown recipe found in their own archives — hence the name "Plough's Tail".
queueDeCharrue.roomStory: The spirit of Flemish farmhouses: sobriety, calm, natural tones. Striped black-and-white linens, soft light, touches of wood and grey. The most peaceful of the three rooms — perfect to truly disconnect.
queueDeCharrue.atmosphere: A room that smells of rest. The kind where you fall asleep early and wake up refreshed.

bieres.pageDescription: Discover our selection of 10 exceptional Belgian beers: Chimay Blue, Westmalle Tripel, Duvel, Orval, Delirium Tremens, Kwak and more. Trappist and abbey, in Bourg-Argental.
bieres.ogDescription: Ten exceptional Belgian beers: Trappist, strong blondes, abbey and special brews. In Bourg-Argental.
bieres.heroImgAlt: Belgian beer selection at Frietkot in Bourg-Argental — Trappist, abbey and special beers, no photographed bottles, premium editorial look
bieres.introTitle: Ten Belgian beers, chosen with care.
bieres.introBody: A Belgian friterie without Belgian beer is a story half told. We have curated a short but honest selection — Trappist, strong blondes, abbey browns, regional specials. Each chosen to pair with a dish, a season, a moment.
bieres.note: Seasonal selection: the beer menu evolves with our deliveries. Ask at the counter — Pascal will guide you.

chimayBleue.description: Deep brown ale, notes of dried fruit, gingerbread and dark chocolate. Refermented in the bottle. Gains complexity with age — some are drunk after ten years.
chimayBleue.pairing: a carbonade flamande, blue cheese, dark chocolate

chimayRouge.description: Amber dubbel, fruity and malty notes, slightly spiced finish. Less powerful than the Bleue, more approachable.
chimayRouge.pairing: the daily special, a charcuterie board, vol-au-vent

westmalleTripel.description: Golden, hopped, lively. Aromas of banana, clove and citrus. Considered by many to be the best tripel in the world.
westmalleTripel.pairing: a carbonade, roast chicken, Belgian fries of course

orval.tagline: The original. The unique. The brett-dry.
orval.description: Pale, dry, heavily hopped with a touch of Brettanomyces that evolves in the bottle. No other beer resembles it. One single beer at Orval — but what a beer.
orval.pairing: Orval cheese, warm goat cheese salad, as an aperitif

duvel.description: Strong golden blonde, dense foam, dry and hoppy finish. Don't trust the colour: 8.5% well hidden. Serve very cold in the tulip glass.
duvel.pairing: mussels, a spicy dish, an aperitif with friends

leffeBlonde.description: Golden blonde, notes of vanilla and citrus, soft finish. A balanced abbey beer, approachable, perfect to begin an evening.
leffeBlonde.pairing: fries with samurai sauce, vol-au-vent, a gratin

delirium.tagline: The pink elephant in the room.
delirium.description: Strong blonde with complex character: alcohol, spices, a long dry finish. Iconic stoneware bottle. The Delirium café in Brussels made it a legend.
delirium.pairing: Liège-style meatballs, aged cheese, rich fries

kwak.description: Strong amber, caramelised notes, malty finish. 18th-century origin: designed to be drunk on horseback, hence the round-bottom glass in its wooden stand.
kwak.pairing: a carbonade flamande, game, aged cheese

paljas.tagline: The lively blonde from the north.
paljas.description: Golden blonde, slightly fruity, refreshing. An accessible beer that pairs wonderfully with rich dishes and moments among friends.
paljas.pairing: a cone of fries with andalouse sauce, a summer day

herbergBrune.description: Amber brown, notes of caramel and dark fruit. Rounded on the palate, slightly sweet finish. Perfect for long evenings.
herbergBrune.pairing: a carbonade, sugar tart, abbey cheese

contact.pageDescription: 7 place d'Armeville, 42220 Bourg-Argental, France. Phone: +33 7 84 42 81 06. Frietkot, authentic Belgian fry shop since 1990.
contact.ogDescription: Find Frietkot at 7 place d'Armeville in Bourg-Argental.
contact.title: Come say hello.
contact.cardBody: Get in touch to book, order or plan your visit.
contact.hours: to be confirmed with the owner

notreHistoire.pageDescription: Since 1990, Pascal has welcomed you to Frietkot. A Belgian fry shop in the Loire where everything is made in-house every day.
notreHistoire.ogDescription: Thirty-five years in the trade. Pascal, owner of Frietkot in Bourg-Argental.
notreHistoire.lead: A fry shop opened in Brussels. Thirty-five years later, the same recipe, in Bourg-Argental.
notreHistoire.p1: Frietkot is first a story of patience. The kind you need to peel potatoes by hand every morning. The kind you need to respect the double cook. The kind you need so every cone leaves the counter at the right heat, with salt and sauce chosen with care.
notreHistoire.p2: Settled for more than thirty years, we serve authentic Belgian fries in the Loire. Not a copy, not a twist — the real thing. With the matching beers, daily specials the way they’re made over there, and hospitality that feels like a village friterie in Belgium.
notreHistoire.p3: Today we’re expanding the experience with an inn. Because a good meal is sometimes the start of a night you want to keep going.
notreHistoire.imgAlt: Pascal, owner of Frietkot in Bourg-Argental, welcomes you to his Belgian fry shop with a smile
